 {"id":"bd-0a43","title":"Split monolithic sqlite.go into focused files","description":"internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go is 1050 lines containing initialization, 20+ CRUD methods, query building, and schema management.

Split into:
- store.go: Store struct & initialization (150 lines)
- bead_queries.go: Bead CRUD (300 lines)
- work_queries.go: Work queries (200 lines)  
- stats_queries.go: Statistics (150 lines)
- schema.go: Schema & migrations (150 lines)
- helpers.go: Common utilities (100 lines)

Impact: Impossible to understand at a glance; hard to find specific functionality; high cognitive load

Effort: 6-8 hours","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:16.520465-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-0d5p","title":"Fix TestRunSync_Timeout failing on macOS","description":"The hooks timeout test fails because exec.CommandContext doesn't properly terminate child processes of shell scripts on macOS. The test creates a hook that runs 'sleep 60' with a 500ms timeout, but it waits the full 60 seconds.

Options to fix:
- Use SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} to create process group and kill the group
- Skip test on darwin with build tag
- Use a different approach for timeout testing

Location: internal/hooks/hooks_test.go:220-253","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:51.771217-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-0e02","title":"Code smell: Inconsistent error handling patterns","description":"Commands use a mix of error handling patterns:
- FatalError()
- FatalErrorRespectJSON()
- fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) + os.Exit(1)
- Silent continue
- Inconsistent resource cleanup (result.Close())

**Example in show.go lines 89-146:**
Different error paths use different patterns, some close resources, some don't.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Document error handling contract
- [ ] Standardize on consistent pattern
- [ ] Use defer for resource cleanup
- [ ] Create helper for common fetch-and-handle pattern
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T18:59:30.118-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.362524-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:02:03.530317-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added LogError, LogErrorRespectJSON, LogNotFound, LogNotFoundRespectJSON helpers to errors.go. Standardized show.go to use these helpers. Updated ERROR_HANDLING.md documentation. Tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0e02","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.683836-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-0fvq","title":"bd doctor should recommend bd prime migration for existing repos","description":"bd doctor should detect old beads integration patterns and recommend migrating to bd prime approach.

## Current behavior
- bd doctor checks if Claude hooks are installed globally
- Doesn't check project-level integration (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md)
- Doesn't recommend migration for repos using old patterns

## Desired behavior
bd doctor should detect and suggest:

1. **Old slash command pattern detected**
   - Check for /beads:* references in AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
   - Suggest: These slash commands are deprecated, use bd prime hooks instead
   
2. **No agent documentation**
   - Check if AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists
   - Suggest: Run 'bd onboard' or 'bd setup claude' to document workflow
   
3. **Old MCP-only pattern**
   - Check for instructions to use MCP tools but no bd prime hooks
   - Suggest: Add bd prime hooks for better token efficiency

4. **Migration path**
   - Show: 'Run bd setup claude to add SessionStart/PreCompact hooks'
   - Show: 'Update AGENTS.md to reference bd prime instead of slash commands'

## Example output

⚠ Warning: Old beads integration detected in CLAUDE.md
  Found: /beads:* slash command references (deprecated)
  Recommend: Migrate to bd prime hooks for better token efficiency
  Fix: Run 'bd setup claude' and update CLAUDE.md

💡 Tip: bd prime + hooks reduces token usage by 80-99% vs slash commands
  MCP mode: ~50 tokens vs ~10.5k for full MCP scan
  CLI mode: ~1-2k tokens with automatic context recovery

## Benefits
- Helps existing repos adopt new best practices
- Clear migration path for users
- Better token efficiency messaging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-12T03:20:25.567748-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:33:23.931274-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:33:23.931274-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-7h7","title":"bd init should stop running daemon to avoid stale cache","description":"When running bd init, any running daemon continues with stale cached data, causing bd stats and other commands to show old counts.

Repro:
1. Have daemon running with 788 issues cached
2. Clean JSONL to 128 issues, delete db, run bd init
3. bd stats still shows 788 (daemon cache)
4. Must manually run bd daemon --stop

Fix: bd init should automatically stop any running daemon before reinitializing.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:26:47.117226-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-7l27","title":"Integrate migration detection into bd doctor","description":"Multiple migration commands (migrate_hash_ids.go, migrate_issues.go, migrate_sync.go, migrate_tombstones.go, migrate.go) have TODOs suggesting integration into 'bd doctor' for automatic migration detection. This would allow bd doctor to detect when migrations are needed and suggest running them.

Files:
- cmd/bd/migrate_hash_ids.go:24
- cmd/bd/migrate_issues.go:15
- cmd/bd/migrate_sync.go:16
- cmd/bd/migrate_tombstones.go:72
- cmd/bd/migrate.go:22","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:32:24.018282-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.353203-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:06:59.401939-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added CheckPendingMigrations to bd doctor, updated existing migration suggestions to use modern bd migrate subcommands"}
-{"id":"bd-7l67","title":"Add bd state and bd set-state helper commands","description":"Convenience commands for the labels-as-state pattern documented in LABELS.md.

`bd state <role> <dimension>` - Query the current value of a state dimension
Example: bd state witness-alpha patrol → outputs: active

`bd set-state <role> <dimension>=<value> [--reason "..."]` - Atomically creates an event bead and updates the label
Example: bd set-state witness-alpha patrol=muted --reason "Investigating stuck polecat"

This would combine the manual steps:
1. bd create "<reason>" -t event --parent <role>
2. bd label remove <role> <dimension>:<old-value>
3. bd label add <role> <dimension>:<new-value>

Into a single atomic operation.

Discovered from: bd-31ae","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T15:46:40.679911-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:50:21.739567-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l67","depends_on_id":"bd-31ae","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T15:46:50.184553-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

`bd state <role> <dimension>` - Query the current value of a state dimension
Example: bd state witness-alpha patrol → outputs: active

`bd set-state <role> <dimension>=<value> [--reason "..."]` - Atomically creates an event bead and updates the label
Example: bd set-state witness-alpha patrol=muted --reason "Investigating stuck polecat"

This would combine the manual steps:
1. bd create "<reason>" -t event --parent <role>
2. bd label remove <role> <dimension>:<old-value>
3. bd label add <role> <dimension>:<new-value>

Into a single atomic operation.

 {"id":"bd-7pwh","title":"HOP-compatible schema additions","description":"Add optional fields to Beads schema to enable future HOP integration.
All fields are backwards-compatible (optional, omitted if empty).

## Reference
See ~/gt/docs/hop/BEADS-SCHEMA-CHANGES.md for full specification.

## P1 Changes (Must Have Before Launch)

### 1. EntityRef type
Structured entity reference that can become HOP URI:
```go
type EntityRef struct {
    Name     string // "polecat/Nux"
    Platform string // "gastown"
    Org      string // "steveyegge"  
    ID       string // "polecat-nux"
}
```

### 2. creator field
Every issue tracks who created it (EntityRef).

### 3. assignee_ref field
Structured form alongside existing string assignee.

### 4. validations array
Track who validated work completion:
```go
type Validation struct {
    Validator *EntityRef
    Outcome   string    // accepted, rejected, revision_requested
    Timestamp time.Time
    Score     *float32  // Future
}
```

## P2 Changes (Should Have)

### 5. work_type field
"mutex" (default) or "open_competition"

### 6. crystallizes field
Boolean - does this work compound (true) or evaporate (false)?

### 7. cross_refs field
Array of URIs to beads in other repos:
- "beads://github/anthropics/claude-code/bd-xyz"

## P3 Changes (Nice to Have)

### 8. skill_vector placeholder
Reserved for future embeddings: []float32

## Implementation Notes
- All fields optional in JSONL serialization
- Empty/null fields omit from output
- No migration needed for existing data
- CLI additions: --creator, --validated-by filters","notes":"Scope reduced after review. P1 only: EntityRef type, creator field, validations array. Deferred: assignee_ref, work_type, crystallizes, cross_refs, skill_vector (YAGNI - semantics unclear, can add later when needed).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:42:39.267984-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00"}

-{"id":"bd-a0cp","title":"Consider using types.Status in merge package for type safety","description":"The merge package uses string for status comparison (e.g., result.Status == closed, issue.Status == StatusTombstone). The types package defines Status as a type alias with validation. While the merge package needs its own Issue struct for JSONL flexibility, it could import and use types.Status for constants to get compile-time type checking. Current code: if left == closed || right == closed. Could be: if left == string(types.StatusClosed). This is low priority since string comparison works correctly. Files: internal/merge/merge.go:44, 488, 501-521","status":"hooked","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:10.690424-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:49:11.434875-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-a0cp","title":"Consider using types.Status in merge package for type safety","description":"The merge package uses string for status comparison (e.g., result.Status == closed, issue.Status == StatusTombstone). The types package defines Status as a type alias with validation. While the merge package needs its own Issue struct for JSONL flexibility, it could import and use types.Status for constants to get compile-time type checking. Current code: if left == closed || right == closed. Could be: if left == string(types.StatusClosed). This is low priority since string comparison works correctly. Files: internal/merge/merge.go:44, 488, 501-521","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:10.690424-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:54:45.570222-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T15:54:45.570222-08:00","close_reason":"Added StatusClosed constant derived from types.StatusClosed and replaced hardcoded strings with the constant for type safety"}

 {"id":"bd-a3sj","title":"RemoveDependency fails on external deps - FK violation in dirty_issues","description":"In dependencies.go:225, RemoveDependency marks BOTH issueID and dependsOnID as dirty. For external refs (e.g., external:project:capability), dependsOnID doesn't exist in the issues table. This causes FK violation since dirty_issues.issue_id has FK constraint to issues.id.

Fix: Check if dependsOnID starts with 'external:' and only mark source issue as dirty, matching the logic in AddDependency (lines 162-170).

Repro: bd dep rm <issue> external:project:capability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:44:51.981138-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:48:29.062424-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:48:29.062424-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-a3sj","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:44:51.982343-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
